tasks.subject: [slack-adapter] [P1] Add shared formatForPlatform() utility to payload-utils.ts
[slack-adapter] [P1] Add Slack schemas and type to shared package
tasks.description:
## Overview

Add the `'slack'` variant to the `AdapterTypeSchema` enum, define `SlackAdapterConfigSchema`, and add it to the config union in `packages/shared/src/relay-adapter-schemas.ts`. Also add the type re-export in `packages/relay/src/types.ts`.

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Update AdapterTypeSchema in packages/shared/src/relay-adapter-schemas.ts

Change the `AdapterTypeSchema` enum to include `'slack'`:

```typescript
export const AdapterTypeSchema = z
  .enum(['telegram', 'webhook', 'claude-code', 'slack', 'plugin'])
  .openapi('AdapterType');
```

### 2. Add SlackAdapterConfigSchema

Add the Slack config schema after the existing `WebhookAdapterConfigSchema` block:

```typescript
export const SlackAdapterConfigSchema = z
  .object({
    botToken: z.string().min(1),
    appToken: z.string().min(1),
    signingSecret: z.string().min(1),
  })
  .openapi('SlackAdapterConfig');

export type SlackAdapterConfig = z.infer<typeof SlackAdapterConfigSchema>;
```

### 3. Update AdapterConfigSchema.config union

Add `SlackAdapterConfigSchema` to the config union in `AdapterConfigSchema`:

```typescript
config: z.union([
  TelegramAdapterConfigSchema,
  WebhookAdapterConfigSchema,
  SlackAdapterConfigSchema,
  z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()),
]),
```

### 4. Add type re-export in packages/relay/src/types.ts

Add the import and re-export for `SlackAdapterConfig`:

```typescript
// In the import block at the top, add SlackAdapterConfig:
import type {
  // ... existing imports ...
  SlackAdapterConfig,
} from '@dorkos/shared/relay-schemas';

// Add re-export alongside the existing config type re-exports:
/** Configuration for the Slack adapter. */
export type { SlackAdapterConfig };
```

### 5. Add type re-export in packages/relay/src/index.ts

In the adapter types section of `packages/relay/src/index.ts`, add `SlackAdapterConfig` to the re-exported types:

```typescript
export type {
  RelayPublisher,
  RelayAdapter,
  AdapterStatus,
  AdapterConfig,
  AdapterContext,
  DeliveryResult,
  TelegramAdapterConfig,
  WebhookAdapterConfig,
  SlackAdapterConfig,
  AdapterRegistryLike,
  PublishResultLike,
} from './types.js';
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `AdapterTypeSchema` accepts `'slack'` as a valid value
- [ ] `SlackAdapterConfigSchema` validates `{ botToken, appToken, signingSecret }` with `.min(1)` on all fields
- [ ] `SlackAdapterConfigSchema` rejects empty strings for any field
- [ ] `SlackAdapterConfigSchema` has `.openapi('SlackAdapterConfig')` annotation
- [ ] `AdapterConfigSchema.config` union includes `SlackAdapterConfigSchema`
- [ ] `SlackAdapterConfig` type is re-exported from both `packages/relay/src/types.ts` and `packages/relay/src/index.ts`
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes with no errors
tasks.activeForm: Adding Slack adapter schemas and types to shared package

tasks.subject: [slack-adapter] [P1] Install @slack/bolt dependency
tasks.description:
## Overview

Add `@slack/bolt` to `packages/relay/package.json` as a production dependency. This is the official Slack SDK that provides Socket Mode support, Web API client, and event handling.

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Install @slack/bolt

```bash
cd packages/relay && pnpm add @slack/bolt
```

### 2. Verify installation

Confirm `@slack/bolt` is listed in `packages/relay/package.json` under `dependencies` and that the lockfile is updated.

### 3. Verify types

Create a temporary TypeScript file to verify the types are available:

```typescript
// Quick smoke check (delete after verification):
import { App } from '@slack/bolt';
import { WebClient } from '@slack/web-api';

// These types should be available from @slack/bolt's transitive deps
type _TestApp = App;
type _TestClient = WebClient;
```

Ensure `pnpm typecheck` passes after adding the dependency.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `@slack/bolt` is listed in `packages/relay/package.json` dependencies
- [ ] `pnpm install` completes without errors
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] The `App` and `WebClient` types from `@slack/bolt` and `@slack/web-api` are importable

tasks.subject: [slack-adapter] [P4] Run full test suite and fix any integration issues
tasks.description:
## Overview

Run the complete test suite across the monorepo to verify the Slack adapter integration doesn't break existing tests. Fix any type errors, lint issues, or test failures.

## Steps

### 1. Run type checking

```bash
pnpm typecheck
```

Verify zero errors across all packages. Pay special attention to:
- `packages/shared` — AdapterTypeSchema now includes 'slack'
- `packages/relay` — new exports compile correctly
- `apps/server` — factory and manager imports resolve

### 2. Run linting

```bash
pnpm lint
```

Verify:
- TSDoc comments on all exported functions
- No `@slack/bolt` imports outside `packages/relay/src/adapters/slack/`
- Import order is correct

### 3. Run all tests

```bash
pnpm test -- --run
```

Verify:
- All existing Telegram adapter tests still pass
- All existing relay core tests still pass
- All new Slack adapter tests pass:
  - `packages/relay/src/adapters/slack/__tests__/inbound.test.ts`
  - `packages/relay/src/adapters/slack/__tests__/outbound.test.ts`
  - `packages/relay/src/adapters/slack/__tests__/slack-adapter.test.ts`
  - `packages/relay/src/lib/__tests__/payload-utils.test.ts` (formatForPlatform additions)

### 4. Run build

```bash
pnpm build
```

Verify all packages build successfully.

### 5. Fix any issues

Common issues to watch for:
- `slackify-markdown` may need type declarations — if so, add `@types/slackify-markdown` or create a `slackify-markdown.d.ts` in packages/relay
- The `@slack/bolt` package may bring in peer dependency warnings — resolve them
- Ensure `AdapterTypeSchema` changes don't break existing adapter config validation tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes with zero errors
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes with zero errors (or only pre-existing warnings)
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes all tests
- [ ] `pnpm build` succeeds
- [ ] No regressions in existing adapter tests
tasks.activeForm: Running full test suite and fixing integration issues
